verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And Joses, who by the Apostles was surnamed Barnabas (which is, being interpreted, The son of consolation), a Levite, and of the country of Cyprus, having land, sold it, and brought the money, and laid it at the Apostles’ feet.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The writer is now about to relate the affair of Ananias and Sapphira, and in order to show that the man’s sin was of the worst description, he first mentions him who performed the virtuous deed; that, there being so great a multitude all doing the same, so great grace, so great miracles, he, taught by none of these, but blinded by covetousness, brought destruction upon his own head.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Having land,—meaning that this was all he possessed,—sold it, and brought the money, and laid it at the Apostles’ feet.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But a certain man named Ananias, with Sapphira his wife, sold a possession, and kept back part of the price, his wife also being privy to it, and brought a certain part, and laid it at the Apostles’ feet.” (ch. v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The aggravating circumstance was, that the sin was concerted, and none other saw what was done.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How came it into the mind of this hapless wretch to commit this crime?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But Peter said, Ananias, why hath Satan filled thine heart to lie to the Holy Ghost, and to keep back part of the price of the land?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe even in this, a great miracle performed, greater far than the former.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Whiles it remained,” says he, “was it not thine own? and after it was sold, was it not in thine own power?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: That is, “Was there any obligation and force? do we constrain you against your will?”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Why hast thou conceived this thing in thine heart? thou hast not lied unto men, but unto God.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And Ananias hearing these words fell down, and gave up the ghost.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This miracle is greater than that of the lame man, in respect of the death inflicted, and the knowing what was in the thought of the heart, even what was done in secret.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And great fear came on all them that heard these things.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And the young men arose, and wound him up, and carried him out, and buried him.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And Peter answered unto her, Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The woman he would fain save, for the man had been the author of the sin: therefore he gives her time to clear herself, and opportunity for repentance, saying, “Tell me whether ye sold the land for so much?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And she said, Yea, for so much.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Then Peter said unto her, How is it that ye have agreed together to tempt the Holy Ghost?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door, and shall carry thee out.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Then she fell down straightway at his feet, and yielded up the ghost; and the young men came in, and found her dead, and, carrying her forth, buried her by her husband.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And great fear came upon all the Church, and upon as many as heard these things.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: After this fear had come upon them, he wrought more miracles; both Peter and the rest; “And by the hands of the Apostles were many signs and wonders wrought among the people; and they were all with one accord in Solomon’s porch.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And of the rest durst no man join himself to them,” i.e. to the Apostles; “but the people magnified them,” i.e. the Jewish people.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If “no man durst join himself unto them,” the Apostles, “there were,” however, “the more added unto the Lord, believers, multitudes both of men and of women, insomuch that they brought out into the streets their impotent folk, and laid them upon couches and beds, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For Peter was the wonderful one, and he to whom they more gave heed both because of his public harangue, the first and the second and the third, and because of the miracle; for he it was that wrought the miracle, the first, the second, the third: for the present miracle was twofold: first, the convicting the thoughts of the heart, and next the inflicting of death at his word of command.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “That at the least the shadow of Peter passing by,” etc. This had not occurred in the history of Christ; but see here what He had told them actually coming to pass, that “they which believe on Me, the works that I do shall they do also; and greater works than these shall they do.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “There came also a multitude out of the cities round about unto Jerusalem, bringing sick folks, and them that were vexed with unclean spirits; and they were healed every one.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And now I would have you observe the way in which their whole life is interwoven.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: First there was despondency on account of Christ taken from them, and then came joy because of the Spirit descending upon them; again, dejection because of the scoffers, and then joy in the result of their own apology.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And here again we find both dejection and gladness.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In that they were become conspicuous, and that God made revelations to them, there was gladness: in that they had cut off some of their own company, there was sadness.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Once more: again there is gladness upon their success, and again sadness by reason of the High Priest.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And so it will be seen to be the case throughout.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And the same will be found to hold in the case of the ancient saints likewise.—But let us look over again what has been said.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “They sold them,” it is written, “and brought the prices, and laid them down at the Apostles’ feet.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (Recapitulation. iv.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: See, my beloved brethren, how instead of leaving the Apostles to sell, they themselves sold, and presented the prices to them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But a certain man named Ananias,” etc. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This history touches Bishops too, and very forcibly.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And the wife of Ananias was privy to the thing done: therefore he examines her.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But perhaps some one will say that he dealt very harshly with her.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What do you mean?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: What harshness?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If for gathering sticks a man is to be stoned, much rather ought he for sacrilege; for this money was become sacred.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: He that has chosen to sell his goods and distribute them, and then withdraws them, is guilty of sacrilege.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But if he is sacrilegious, who resumes from his own, much more he who takes from what is not his own.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And do not think that because the consequence is not now the same, the crime will go unpunished.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Do you see that this is the charge brought against Ananias, that having made the money sacred, he afterwards secreted it?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Couldest thou not, said Peter, after selling thy land, use the proceeds as thine own?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Wast thou forbidden?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Wherefore after thou hadst promised it?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: See how at the very beginning, the devil made his attack; in the very midst of such signs and wonders, how this man was hardened!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Something of the same kind had happened upon a time in the Old Testament.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The son of Charmi coveted the devoted thing: for observe there also what vengeance ensues upon the sin.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Sacrilege, beloved, is a most grievous crime, insulting, and full of contempt.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: We neither obliged thee to sell, the Apostle says, nor to give thy money when thou hadst sold; of thine own free choice thou didst it; why hast thou then stolen from the sacred treasury?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “Why,” he says, “hath Satan filled thine heart?” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Well, if Satan did the thing, why is the man made guilty of it?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For admitting the influence of the devil, and being filled with it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: You will say, they ought to have corrected him.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But he would not have received correction; for he that has seen such things as he had seen, and is none the better, would certainly be none the better for anything else that could be done; the matter was not one to be simply passed over: like a gangrene, it must be cut out, that it might not infect the rest of the body.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: As it is, both the man himself is benefitted in regard that he is not left to advance further in wickedness, and the rest, in that they are made more earnest; otherwise the contrary would have ensued.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In the next place, Peter proves him guilty, and shows that the deed was not hidden from him, and then pronounces the sentence.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But wherefore, upon what purpose hast thou done this?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Didst thou wish to keep it?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Thou oughtest to have kept it all along, and never to have professed to give it.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The sacrilege, beloved, is a grievous one.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For another, it may be, coveted what was not his own: but it was at thy discretion to keep what was thine own.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Why then didst thou first make it sacred, and then take it?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Out of excessive contempt hast thou done this.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The deed does not admit of pardon, it is past pleading for.—Therefore let it be no stumbling-block to any, if at present also there are sacrilegious persons.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If there were such persons then, much more now, when evils are many.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But let us “rebuke them before all, that others also may fear.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Judas was sacrilegious, but it was no stumbling-block to the disciples.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Do you see how many evils spring from love of money?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And great fear, it is said, came on all them that heard these things.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: That man was punished, and others profited thereby.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Not without cause.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet, signs had been wrought before: true, but there was not such a sense of fear.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So true is that saying, “The Lord is known by executing judgments.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The same thing had occurred in the case of the Ark: Uzzah was punished and fear came upon the rest.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But in that instance the king through fear removed from him the Ark; but here the disciples became more earnestly heedful. [“And it was about the space of three hours after, when his wife, not knowing what was done, came in,” etc.] (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But observe how Peter, instead of sending for her, waited till she entered; and how none of the others durst carry out the intelligence.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Such the teacher’s awfulness, such the disciples’ reverence, such the obedience!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “An interval of three hours,”—and yet the woman did not hear of it, and none of those present reported it, although there was time enough for it to be noised abroad; but they were afraid.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This circumstance the Evangelist relates with wonder even, when he says, “Not knowing what was done, came in.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And Peter answered unto her,” etc. (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And yet she might have perceived even from this that Peter knew the secret.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For why, having questioned none other, does he question you?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Was it not clear that he asked because he knew?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But so great was her hardness, it would not let her attempt to evade the guilt; and with great confidence she replied; for she thought she was speaking only to a man.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The aggravation of the sin was, that they committed it as with one soul, just as upon a settled compact between them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “How is it that ye have agreed together,” he said, “to tempt the Spirit of the Lord? behold, the feet of them which have buried thy husband are at the door.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: First he makes her learn the sin, and then shows that she will justly suffer the same punishment with her husband, since she has committed the same wickedness: “And they shall carry thee out.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And she fell down straightway at his feet,” for she was standing near him, “and yielded up the ghost.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So entirely by their own act had they invited upon themselves the vengeance!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Who after that would not be struck with awe? who would not fear the Apostle? who would not marvel? who not be afraid?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And they were with one accord, all of them in Solomon’s porch,” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: 12) no longer in a house, but having occupied the very Temple, they there passed their time!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: No longer they guarded themselves against touching the unclean; nay, without scruple they handled the dead.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And observe how, while to their own people they are severe, against the aliens they do not exercise their power.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But the people,” he says, “magnified them.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And as he had mentioned their being “in Solomon’s porch,” that you may not wonder how the multitude allowed this, he tells us that they did not dare even to approach them: for “no man,” he says, “durst join himself unto them.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “But believers were the more added unto the Lord, multitudes both of men and women: insomuch that they brought forth the sick into the streets, and laid them on beds and couches, that at the least the shadow of Peter passing by might overshadow some of them.” (v.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Great faith, surpassing what had been shown in the case of Christ.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How comes this?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Because Christ declared: “And greater works than these shall he do, because I go unto My Father.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And these things the people do, while the Apostles remain there, and are not moving about from place to place: also from other places they were all bringing [their sick] on beds and couches: and from all quarters accrued to them fresh tribute of wonder; from them that believed, from them that were healed, from him that was punished; from their boldness of speech towards those (their adversaries), from the virtuous behavior of the believers: for certainly the effect produced was not owing to the miracles only.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For though the Apostles themselves modestly ascribe it all to this cause, declaring that they did these things in the name of Christ, yet at the same time the life and noble conduct of the men helped to produce this effect.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “And believers were more added unto the Lord, multitudes both of men and women.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Observe, how he now no longer tells the number of them that believe; at such a rate was the faith making way even to an immense multitude, and so widely was the Resurrection proclaimed.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: So then “the people magnified them:” but they were now no longer lightly to be despised as once they were: for in a little moment, at a single turn of the scale, such have been the effects produced by the fisherman and by the publican!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Earth was become a heaven, for manner of life, for boldness of speech, for wonders, for all besides; like Angels were they looked upon with wonder: all unconcerued for ridicule, for threats, for perils: compassionate were they, and beneficent; some of them they succoured with money, and some with words, and some with healing of their bodies and of their souls; no kind of healing (πἅν εἶδος ἰατρείας) but they accomplished.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Peter all but pleads for himself, when at the point to inflict the punishment, and at the same time gives a lesson to the rest.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For because the act would seem exceeding stern, therefore it is that he does so much in the case.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In respect of the woman also the process of judgment was terrible.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But see how many evils grow out of the sacrilege: covetousness, contempt of God, impiety; and upon these too he pleaded for himself before the assembly, in that he did not immediately proceed to punishment, but first exposed the sin.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: None groaned, none lamented, all were terrified.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For as their faith increased, the signs also were multiplied, and great was the fear among their own company: for the things which are from without do not so militate (πολεμεἵ) against our peace, as do the acts of our own people.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If we be firmly joined together, no warfare will be hard: but the mischief would be the being divided and broken up.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Now they went about in the public place: with boldness they attacked even the market, and in the midst of enemies they prevailed, and that saying was fulfilled, “Be Thou Ruler in the midst among Thine enemies.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: This was a greater miracle, that they, arrested, cast into prison, should do such acts as these!

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If those for lying suffered such things, what shall not the perjured suffer?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Because she simply affirmed, “Yea, for so much,” ye see what she suffered.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Bethink you then; they that swear and forswear themselves, of what should they be worthy?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It comes in opportunely to-day even from the Old Testament to show you the heinousness of perjury.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: “There was,” it says, “a flying sickle, ten cubits in breadth.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The “flying” betokens the swift advent of the vengeance which pursues oaths; that it is many cubits in length and breadth, signifies the force and magnitude of the woes; that it comes flying “from heaven,” is to show that the vengeance comes from the judgment-seat on high: that it is in the form of a sickle,” denotes the inevitableness of the doom: for just as the sickle, where it comes and has hooked the neck, is not drawn back with nothing but itself, but with the head reaped off, even so the vengeance which comes upon the swearers is severe, and will not desist until it have completed its work.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: But if we swear and escape, let us not be confident; this is but to our woe.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For what think ye?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How many, since Ananias and Sapphira, have dared the same with them?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: How is it then, say you, that they have not met with the same fate?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Not because it was allowed in them, but because they are reserved for a greater punishment.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For those who often sin and are not punished, have greater reason to fear and dread than if they were punished.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For the vengeance is increased for them by their present impunity and the long-suffering of God.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Then let us not look to this, that we are not punished; but let us consider whether we have not sinned: if sinning we are not punished, we have the more reason to tremble.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Say, if you have a slave, and you only threaten him, and do not beat him; when is he most in fear, when most inclined to run away?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Is is not when you only threaten him?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And hence we advise each other not to be continually using threats, thereby choosing rather to agitate the mind by the terror, and lacerating it worse than with blows.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For in the one instance the punishment is momentary, but in the other it is perpetual.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: If then no one feels the stroke of the sickle, do not look to this, but rather let each consider whether he commits such sins.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Many like things are done now as were done before the Flood, yet no flood has been sent: because there is a hell threatened, and vengeance.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Many sin as the people did in Sodom, yet no rain of fire has been poured down; because a river of fire is prepared.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Many go the lengths of Pharaoh; yet they have not fared like Pharaoh, they have not been drowned in a Red Sea: for the sea that awaits them, is the sea of the bottomless pit, where the punishment is not accompanied with insensibility, where there is no suffocation to end all, but in ever lengthened torture, in burning, in strangling, they are consumed there.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Many have offended like the Israelites, but no serpents have devoured them: there awaits them the worm that never dieth.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Many have been like Gehazi, yet they have not been struck with leprosy: for instead of leprosy, it remains for them to be cut asunder, and numbered among the hypocrites.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Many have both sworn and forsworn; but if they have indeed escaped, let us not be confident: the gnashing of teeth awaits them.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Yea, here too they will suffer many grievous woes, though, it may be, not immediately, but after further transgressions, that the vengeance may be the greater; for even we often set out at first with small sins, and then through great offences lose all.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Therefore when you see anything happening to you, call to mind that particular sin of yours.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: The sons of Jacob are an example of this.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Remember Joseph’s brothers; they had sold their brother, they had even attempted to slay him; nay, they had slain him, as far as inclination went; they had deceived and grieved the old man; they suffered nothing.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: After many years they are brought into extreme peril, and now they are put in remembrance of this their sin.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Exceeding wisely is this circumstance brought in.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Hear what they say: “We are verily guilty concerning our brother.”

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: In this manner then do thou also, when anything happens, say, We are verily guilty, because we have not obeyed Christ; because we have sworn; my much swearing, and my false swearing, has fallen upon my own head.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Confess thou; since they also confessed, and were saved.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: For what though the punishment follow not immediately?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Since Ahab also did not immediately after his sin in the matter of Naboth suffer that vengeance which he yet at last suffered.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: (1 Kings xxi.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: And what is the reason of this?

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: God sets thee a time, in which to wash thyself clean; but if thou persist, at last He will send down the vengeance.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: You have seen the fate of liars.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Consider what is the fate of false swearers, consider, and desist.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: It is impossible a swearer should not forswear himself, whether he will or not; and no perjurer can be saved.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: One false oath sufficeth to finish all, to draw down upon us the whole measure of vengeance.

verses.book: A Commentary on the Acts of the Apostles
verses.text: Let us then take heed to ourselves, that we may escape the punishment due to this offence, and be deemed worthy of the loving kindness of God, through the grace and mercies of His only-begotten Son, with Whom to the Father and the Holy Ghost be glory, power, and honor, now and ever, and world without end.
